framing: HEADLINE: calendar_true — the US menu index Granger-leads CPI at an exact 1-month lag (F=4.20, analytic p=0.0499, n=31), using only observed menu months and true 1-month CPI changes. ROBUSTNESS: interp_ffill shows the result strengthens (not weakens) when single-month menu gaps are filled causally with past information only; interp_1mo (midpoint) agrees but carries a look-ahead caveat. FOOTNOTE: strict_pairs is directionally consistent but uninformative at n=10. The original spec is deprecated: its p=0.021 was partly an artifact of gap-mixed CPI changes.

original.note: previous-obs lag, gap-mixed CPI changes (former headline pipeline). Superseded by calendar_true: intersecting CPI to menu months before differencing mislabeled the lag and mixed 1-9 month CPI changes. Do not cite as headline.

calendar_true.note: exact 1-month lags, full-calendar CPI changes, all menu obs used, no imputation

strict_pairs.note: exact 1-month lags, rows where menu(t) AND menu(t-1) both observed; stricter than the equation requires and underpowered at n=10 — directionally consistent, uninformative

interp_1mo.note: single-month menu gaps midpoint-interpolated, then exact 1-month lags; CAVEAT: filled months embed half-weight look-ahead — prefer interp_ffill

interp_ffill.note: single-month menu gaps forward-filled (causal, past info only), then exact 1-month lags
